free_text: Charge, mass, and angular distributions in 20-Ne
induced fission of 208-Pb
Radiochemistry division
For charge, mass, and angular distribution studies,
enriched 208-Pb (99.86%) target of thickness 2 mg/cm2
was bombarded with 145 MeV 20Ne6+ beam.
For charge and mass distribution studies, the target
was covered from both sides with pure Al catcher foils
of thickness 6.75 mg/cm2 to collect the recoiling
fission products in the forward as well as backward
directions.
Irradiation was carried out for about 4 hour
for product yield measurements and 42 hours for angular
distributions measurements.
For angular distributions.
The beam energy was degraded to 116 MeV by the
backward aluminum foil, so that the average energy in
the target was 114.4 MeV.
For angular distribution studies, the beam
energy was degraded by 6.75 mg/cm2 (effective thickness
9.55 mg/cm2) superpure aluminum foil so that the
average energy in the target was 102.1 MeV.
Beam current was measured from a beam dump
placed after the target. The beam current was around
10 pnA.
The decay of the fission products was followed
for about 10 days. The gamma-ray spectra were
collected over a period of about 20 days to measure
angular distributions.
-By authors. The measurements were carried out using
recoil catcher technique followed by the off-line
gamma-ray assay of fission products.
The uncertainties on the formation cross
sections are quoted at one sigma level.
Variance of mass distribution for various fissioning
systems.
From A.Goswami. Same data presented in table II
Data tables were received.
Additional data tables were received.
Last checking has been done.

The cross section was calculated using unchange

Any analysis of the uncertainty is not

It is atomic number of the isotope whose
cumulative or independent yield has been used to
calculate the mass yield.

It is atomic number of the isotope whose
cumulative or independent yield has been used to
calculate the mass yield.
-By authors.  Conversion of laboratory angular
distribution to center of mass angular distribution
requires the knowledge of kinetic energy of fission
fragments. Kinetic energies were calculated using the
prescription of Rossner et al.
-By authors. All angular distribution were fitted by
A+B*cos**2(ang-cm).
- By compiler. Sign of cosine is unknown.
